title: A Common-place Book of the Fifteenth Century, Containing a Religious Play and Poetry, Legal Forms, and Local Accounts
summary: The pinned General Corpus XML is a normalized version of a raw University of Michigan OCR/TEI encoding of Lucy Toulmin Smith's 1886 edition-defined selection from the Book of Brome. The raw U-M package, public U-M item, pinned serialization, and May 2026 Medictionary successor are distinct digital layers. Smith selected current Yale articles 1–4 and 6–28, rearranged them into three topical parts, reused three antecedent publications, and collated several components directly or through nineteenth-century editions. The complete Yale IIIF object carries the underlying selected manuscript contents; Vernon, Ashmole 61, Trinity B.11.24, Cotton Caligula A II, Sloane 513, and their scans carry supporting versions or analogues only. Access and rights are recorded separately for historical text, repository XML, scan carriers, manuscript images, and successor markup.
review_notes: The catalog subject is the 1886 edition-defined edited selection, not one medieval work or the whole Yale codex.
No same_as relations are used. Version and provenance directions are qualified by exact comparison evidence.
Yale 'Completely digitized' is bounded to all 167 provider-exposed surfaces, including the native-full spine exception.
Every one of the 56 cached files has explicit target_work_presence; absent supporting objects have no work_portion.
The CME root Unlicense is evidence but does not resolve rights in inherited XML/data/markup.

entities.label: General Corpus pinned XML serialization for ajd3529
entities.description: The normalized XML artifact supplied through the pinned Kyvero-Press/CME submodule. It is materially distinct from the raw U-M package XML, the public U-M item, and the Medictionary successor.
notes: 340,832 bytes; well formed. The root CME/LICENSE describes software under the Unlicense, but path-specific applicability to inherited XML/data/markup was not established.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME public electronic item AJD3529.0001.001
entities.description: The public Corpus of Middle English item created by OCR and Level 1 TEI-in-Libraries encoding, with 188 linked page images.

entities.label: Raw University of Michigan package and XML for AJD3529.0001.001
entities.description: Internet Archive's noindex preservation package containing the raw U-M XML, plain text, header, 188 TIFF page images, and package metadata. The embedded XML is a distinct serialization from the pinned repository file.
entities.reproduction_format: Raw Michigan Book ZIP: XML, TXT, HDR, 188 TIFF images, and carrier metadata
notes: Embedded raw XML: 333,119 bytes, SHA-256 82782afb7e76e1c4b7af84c67f99bf39b40c686ca645a15aa498cfa26dc5e003.

entities.label: Medictionary successor XML for ajd3529
entities.description: May 2026 successor serialization with a common schema and minimal TEI-like header. Its normalized BODY text, PB references, and folio milestones match the pinned repository text, while wrapper, element names, and front-title punctuation differ.
notes: A literal extraneous '>' precedes 'A Religious Play…' in the front title.

entities.label: Lucy Toulmin Smith's A Common-place Book of the Fifteenth Century (1886)
entities.description: The edition-defined composite selection: Smith rearranged selected Book of Brome contents into poetry, manorial law, and private accounts, supplied introductions and notes, and collated principal literary texts.

bibliographic.citation: A Common-place Book of the Fifteenth Century, Containing a Religious Play and Poetry, Legal Forms, and Local Accounts, printed from the original manuscript at Brome Hall by Lady Caroline Kerrison, ed. Lucy Toulmin Smith (London: Trübner & Co.; Norwich: Agas H. Goose, 1886).
bibliographic.title: A Common-place Book of the Fifteenth Century, Containing a Religious Play and Poetry, Legal Forms, and Local Accounts

entities.label: Google/Harvard scan of Smith's 1886 edition
entities.description: A complete 203-page Google Books PDF distributed through Internet Archive, with OCR, scandata, and item metadata.
entities.reproduction_format: PDF, OCR text, scan-data XML, and item metadata JSON

entities.label: Book of Brome, Beinecke MS 365
entities.description: Late-fifteenth-century East Anglian paper miscellany with later Robert Melton additions. Smith's edition selects current catalog articles 1–4 and 6–28 and rearranges them.

date_statements.label: Melton additions
date_statements.value: late fifteenth to early sixteenth century, c. 1499–1508

holding.notes: The Yale IIIF metadata gives the call-number variant Beinecke MS 365 1.

physical_description.extent: ff. ii + 78 + ii; modern foliation 1–81 includes flyleaves
physical_description.format: Composite miscellany in original late-fifteenth-century limp parchment binding
notes: Twelve leaves and one flyleaf are missing; the catalog identifies no literary loss after the missing leaves at ff. 7 and 41.
Current catalog describes 29 articles.

entities.label: Yale complete exposed IIIF object for Beinecke MS 365

entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 3 manifest and 167 JPEG derivatives

entities.label: Joe Stadolnik, 'The Brome Abraham and Isaac and Impersonal Compilation' (2019)

scope.notes: Normalized BODY text, PB refs, milestones, and tag sequence agree; attributes and whitespace differ, so this is not same_as.
Identifiers and headers connect the raw package to the public item; the provider's current delivered serialization may differ.
BODY identity is exact after whitespace normalization, but wrapper/front formatting differs; no same_as claim.
Smith's editorial matter and order are not present in the manuscript; articles 5 and 29 are excluded.

rights.component: Inherited repository XML/data/markup
rights.basis: CME/LICENSE contains the Unlicense for software, but no path-specific statement was verified applying it to inherited XML, data, or markup.
notes: Do not infer that the root software license resolves rights in the XML artifact.

rights.component: Underlying digitized edition/text as presented by U-M
rights.basis: Current U-M item/search presentation asserts No Copyright - United States for the item.

rights.component: Legacy raw package XML/header and carrier files
rights.basis: The 2006 header says 'Where applicable, subject to copyright' and that other distribution restrictions may apply. This legacy boilerplate is retained separately from the current NoC-US item assertion.

rights.component: 1886 edition text and editorial matter
rights.basis: Published in 1886; the Google scan identifies the book as public domain, and the current U-M item asserts NoC-US.

rights.component: Google/Harvard scan carrier and digitization
rights.basis: Google's front matter identifies the underlying book as public domain but requests noncommercial use, attribution, and no automated querying; these carrier/service terms are separate from textual copyright.

rights.component: May 2026 successor formatting and markup
rights.basis: Medictionary says raw CME files are freely downloadable with no restrictions on use or reuse, but no formal license or path-specific copyright statement for the successor formatting/markup was identified.

rights.component: Medieval manuscript text
rights.basis: The medieval text predates modern copyright; this does not determine rights in Yale's images or site services.

rights.component: Sloane-derived first line, cast numerals, and variant readings published in Smith's 1886 dice-prognostic text
rights.basis: Smith states that she supplied the first line from Sloane MS 513, transferred its marginal cast numerals, and prints Sloane variant readings in the 1886 edition. This United States conclusion is limited to that nineteenth-century published material.
notes: No United States conclusion is asserted for unquoted Sloane wording. United Kingdom status is unresolved pending passage-specific pre-1989 publication analysis; medieval age alone does not defeat the 2039 rule.

rights.component: Cotton Ipotis ff. 79v–83r, Fifteen Signs ff. 89r–91r, and Owayne ff. 91v–95r as transcribed in nineteenth-century editions
rights.basis: The exact supporting Cotton passages were transcribed in Horstmann 1881 (Ipotis), Varnhagen 1880 (Fifteen Signs), and Kölbing 1877/1882 (Owayne and corrections). This public-domain conclusion is limited to the United States and the nineteenth-century published witness text.
notes: United Kingdom status is unresolved pending confirmation that each exact witness passage was validly made available before 1989; medieval age alone does not defeat the 2039 rule.

rights.component: Vernon Epotys ff. 296v–297v as transcribed by Horstmann in 1881
rights.basis: Horstmann's 1881 edition transcribes the exact Vernon supporting passage at pp. 341–348. This public-domain conclusion is limited to the United States and that nineteenth-century published witness text.
notes: United Kingdom status is unresolved pending confirmation that the exact witness passage was validly made available before 1989; medieval age alone does not defeat the 2039 rule.

rights.component: Ashmole Life of St Margaret ff. 145r–150v as transcribed by Horstmann in 1881
rights.basis: Horstmann's 1881 edition transcribes the exact Ashmole supporting passage at pp. 236–241. This public-domain conclusion is limited to the United States and that nineteenth-century published witness text.
notes: United Kingdom status is unresolved pending confirmation that the exact witness passage was validly made available before 1989; medieval age alone does not defeat the 2039 rule.

rights.component: Trinity Fifteen Signs, part II ff. 27v–31r, as transcribed in Furnivall's 1867 edition
rights.basis: Furnivall's 1867 edition prints the exact Trinity supporting poem at pp. 118–125 from W. Aldis Wright's transcript. This public-domain conclusion is limited to the United States and that nineteenth-century published witness text.
notes: United Kingdom status is unresolved pending confirmation that the exact witness passage was validly made available before 1989; medieval age alone does not defeat the 2039 rule.

rights.component: Seven Wise Counsels wording and comparison reading published or quoted by Smith in 1886, insofar as attributable to Harley MS 116
rights.basis: Smith's 1886 edition identifies Harley MS 116 as a comparison witness and publishes the shared text and 'variaunce' reading, but does not identify which Harley manuscript supplied that reading. The United States conclusion is limited to the material actually published or quoted in 1886.
notes: No United States conclusion is asserted for unquoted Harley MS 116 wording. United Kingdom status is unresolved pending passage-specific pre-1989 publication analysis; medieval age alone does not defeat the 2039 rule.

rights.component: Seven Wise Counsels wording and comparison reading published or quoted by Smith in 1886, insofar as attributable to Harley MS 2251
rights.basis: Smith's 1886 edition identifies Harley MS 2251 as a comparison witness and publishes the shared text and 'variaunce' reading, but does not identify which Harley manuscript supplied that reading. The United States conclusion is limited to the material actually published or quoted in 1886.
notes: No United States conclusion is asserted for unquoted Harley MS 2251 wording. United Kingdom status is unresolved pending passage-specific pre-1989 publication analysis; medieval age alone does not defeat the 2039 rule.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Book of Brome in manuscript order, current Yale articles 1–29
editorial_practices.representation: Current articles 1–4 and 6–28 rearranged into three topical parts and twenty-four edition sections
editorial_practices.description: Smith states that she moved the contents out of manuscript order for convenience and unity. Current article 5 and article 29 are not printed.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Principal literary texts and comparison versions
editorial_practices.representation: Line numbering, chief variants, bracketed supplies, and selected corrections
editorial_practices.description: Smith compared principal poems with manuscripts or prior printed versions; direct and mediated sources are distinguished in relations.
editorial_practices.scope: Dice, Lydgate, Adrian/Epotys, Fifteen Signs, St Patrick, and St Margaret

editorial_practices.representation: Expanded forms printed in italics, except that Latin-charter and account expansions are not uniformly distinguished
editorial_practices.description: Smith's preface explains the expansion convention and its exceptions.

evidence.citation: General Corpus pinned source XML for ajd3529.
evidence.summary: Supplies identifier, title/source statement, 2006 encoding declaration, legacy availability wording, edition text, page and folio milestones, and editorial notes.

evidence.citation: Kyvero-Press/CME pinned git state for ajd3529.xml.
evidence.summary: Pins the exact normalized repository serialization.

evidence.citation: Kyvero-Press/CME root LICENSE (Unlicense wording for software).
evidence.summary: States that the software is free and unencumbered; does not expressly identify inherited XML/data/markup as covered.

evidence.citation: U.S. Copyright Office, 'Certain Unpublished, Unregistered Works Enter Public Domain' (13 January 2003).
evidence.locator: works neither published nor registered as of 1 January 1978; section 303 transition and 1 January 2003 public-domain threshold
evidence.summary: Explains the United States transition from perpetual common-law protection for unpublished works to federal terms, including the rule for qualifying works by authors who died before 1933 and the exception for works published by 31 December 2002.

evidence.citation: UK Intellectual Property Office, 'Copyright Notice: Duration of copyright (term)' (15 January 2021).
evidence.locator: Exceptions to the general rules: Pre-1989 unpublished works (the '2039' rule)
evidence.summary: Explains that some literary, dramatic, and musical works unpublished at the end of 1988 remain protected in the United Kingdom through 31 December 2039 even when their authors died centuries ago.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse, AJD3529.0001.001.

open_questions.question: Does the CME root Unlicense apply to inherited XML/data/markup at CME/source/CME_phase_1-2/ajd3529.xml?
next_steps: Seek path-specific repository documentation or rights-holder clarification before assigning an open license to the XML artifact.
notes: Until resolved, repository-artifact XML rights remain unknown.

open_questions.question: Does 'Originally printed for private circulation' identify a distinct earlier issue or state?
next_steps: Compare multiple physical copies and consult publisher or bibliographic records for issue/state evidence.

open_questions.question: Does the 1 May 1492 receipt in the main scribe's hand record an actual transaction or a draft/model text?
next_steps: Retain both interpretations unless new codicological or archival evidence resolves the function.

open_questions.question: Is Smith's 'bd. i, p. 186' a typographical error for Englische Studien VII, pp. 181–182?
next_steps: Check Smith's working papers or another authorial correction; until then label the emendation as inference.

open_questions.question: How exactly does Kölbing's legacy Cotton f. 89 locator crosswalk to the current catalog's Owayne ff. 91v–95r?
next_steps: Consult the physical manuscript or a foliation history/reproduction to document the crosswalk.

open_questions.question: Which of Harley 116 ff. 124r–125r and Harley 2251 ff. 168r–168v (Smith's legacy f. 173) supplied Smith's 'variaunce' reading?
next_steps: Collate both witnesses when images or reproductions are available.

open_questions.question: Which exact supporting passages in Sloane MS 513, Cotton MS Caligula A II, the Vernon Manuscript, Ashmole 61, Trinity B.11.24, Harley MS 116, and Harley MS 2251 were validly made available to the public before 1989 for United Kingdom copyright-duration purposes?
next_steps: Audit each nineteenth-century edition or quotation against the exact witness passage and establish whether its dissemination constitutes valid publication or other making-available under the applicable United Kingdom rules.
Until that passage-level history is established, keep the supporting-witness public-domain conclusions limited to evidenced United States scopes and treat United Kingdom status as unresolved.
notes: The UK IPO warns that medieval age alone does not establish public-domain status when exact literary witness text remained unpublished at the end of 1988.

open_questions.question: What formal rights status applies specifically to Medictionary's May 2026 wrapper and formatting?
next_steps: Request a path-specific license statement from the provider if redistribution beyond the stated raw-file reuse permission is contemplated.
